headline: Pennsylvania's new tax-sale notice law is in effect; no bill status changes since yesterday as the mid-summer legislative pause holds the docket static.
paragraphs: The most recent state signing in the ledger is Pennsylvania HB 96 (Act 27 of 2026, signed July 12), which amends the Real Estate Tax Sale Law to let property owners designate a third party — an attorney, family member, or property manager — to receive delinquent-tax notices alongside the owner of record. For developers and lenders managing large residential portfolios, the practical consequence is immediate: a designated-agent mechanism now exists to catch delinquency notices that would otherwise be lost at a stale owner-of-record address, reducing the risk of surprise tax sales on properties managed by someone other than the titleholder. Portfolio compliance teams should incorporate the designation filing into their standard intake workflow now that the law is operative.

No substantive changes appeared in the bill data between yesterday's refresh and today's. The two most consequential federal enactments — the 21st Century ROAD to Housing Act (P.L. 119-101, signed July 11) and the Homebuyers Privacy Protection Act (P.L. 119-36, signed September 2025) — continue to govern; the next milestone on the ROAD Act is HUD rulemaking on FHA loan-limit increases and HOME program eligibility. In Washington, D.C., the FY2027 Budget Support Emergency Act (B26-0724) was retained by the Council on July 14, keeping the District's real property tax and development provisions on schedule for the October fiscal year start. Florida's constitutional property-tax amendments passed in the Sixth Special Session (HJR 1-F, enacted June 16) head to the November ballot; lenders underwriting Florida income properties should begin scenario-modeling the revenue-cap and assessment-increase-limit provisions that would take effect upon voter approval.

Three arenas dominate the mid-summer pending watch list. Federally, the LIHTC expansion bill (H.R. 2725), the workforce housing tax credit (H.R. 8626), and the office-to-residential conversion credit (H.R. 2410) are all queued in House Ways and Means — movement on any of them in the fall session would open new tax-equity financing structures across the pipeline. In Pennsylvania, the anti-predatory listing-contract bill (H.B. 986) sits in Senate Urban Affairs after passing the House; it mirrors the trajectory of New York's S06361, signed in December 2025, and a parallel Pennsylvania enactment would require commission-disclosure restructuring statewide. In New York, the Deed Protection Act (A09554) passed the Assembly in May and is now in Senate Judiciary — its passage would bar foreclosure on any mortgage the lender knows or should know rests on a fraudulently obtained deed, a direct underwriting and title-search implication for lenders on properties with any chain-of-title question.
